source: Rider-Waite-Smith (A.E. Waite, Pictorial Key to the Tarot, 1911) — public domain

cards.name: The Fool
cards.upright: Folly, mania, extravagance, intoxication, delirium, or in the exalted sense the beginnings of a journey toward the spirit. With caution it signifies the superconsciousness in which the soul ventures forth naked and unafraid.
cards.reversed: Hesitancy, negligence, apathy, or the nullification of the journey before it begins. A reckless leap taken without heed of the precipice, or conversely a refusal to move at all.
cards.desc: A gaily-dressed youth stands at a cliff's edge, a small dog leaping at his heels, a wand over his shoulder, eyes raised to the sun.

cards.name: The Magician
cards.upright: Skill, diplomacy, sleight of hand, the magus who draws down power from above and directs it through the four elemental instruments laid upon the table. Mastery of the material world through focused will.
cards.reversed: Weakness of will, duplicity, roguery, a knave, or the misuse of occult power. The physician of olden time who has become the mountebank.
cards.desc: A magus stands before a table bearing cup, pentacle, sword and wand; his right hand raises a wand toward heaven while the left points to earth; an infinity symbol floats above his brow.

cards.name: The High Priestess
cards.upright: Secrets, mystery, the unrevealed future, the woman who interests the Querent. She is Isis, Sheba, Artemis — the guardian of the threshold between manifest and hidden worlds.
cards.reversed: Morbidity, moral shyness, surface knowledge silencing the deeper voice, or the wisdom that is withheld when it should be shared.
cards.desc: A veiled priestess sits between dark and light pillars, a crescent moon at her feet, the scroll of the Law in her lap, a tapestry of pomegranates behind her.

cards.name: The Empress
cards.upright: Fruitfulness, action, initiative, the matrix of the natural world. The great Mother, Demeter, who brings forth wheat and children, art and industry, in their season.
cards.reversed: Light, truth, the unraveling of involved matters, or in the lower sense sterility, indecision, and the dissipation of creative force.
cards.desc: A crowned empress sits in a field of ripe wheat wearing a gown patterned with pomegranates; Venus's symbol glows upon her heart, a cushioned throne before a forest stream.

cards.name: The Emperor
cards.upright: Stability, power, protection, a great personage, the father who confers rank and patrimony. The patterning intelligence that gives form to the Empress's fertility.
cards.reversed: Immobility, tyranny, bereavement, or the domination that crushes rather than protects, leading to ruin.
cards.desc: A bearded emperor in armor sits upon a stone throne decorated with ram's heads, holding an orb and sceptre, a barren mountain range behind him.

cards.name: The Hierophant
cards.upright: Mercy, weakness, servitude, mercy toward the weak. The outward teacher, pontiff, and keeper of ceremonies — the channel through which esoteric truth reaches the many.
cards.reversed: Good-natured weakness, servitude, captivity, or an over-rigid orthodoxy that mistakes the vessel for the truth it carries.
cards.desc: A crowned pontiff in triple-tiered tiara sits between two temple pillars, raising a hand in blessing while two tonsured acolytes kneel before him.

cards.name: The Lovers
cards.upright: Attraction, love, beauty, trials overcome, the union of equals. The temptation of the lower by the higher and the choice that reveals character.
cards.reversed: Failure, foolish designs, estrangement, or the marriage of incompatibles that can only end in divorce of values.
cards.desc: Beneath a radiant angel, a naked man and woman stand beneath the trees of life and knowledge, a serpent twined about the roots.

cards.name: The Chariot
cards.upright: Succour, providence, the chariot that carries the soul onward through trial. Victory is won not by brute force but by the driver who masters the opposing beasts of his nature.
cards.reversed: Defeat, riot, dispute, litigation, the charioteer whose strength has turned against itself, leading to downfall.
cards.desc: A princely warrior stands in a canopied chariot drawn by two sphinxes, one black and one white; a canopy of stars floats above him.

cards.name: Strength
cards.upright: Power, force, courage, fortitude in the face of adversity. Not the strength that strikes but the strength that tames the lion within by gentleness.
cards.reversed: Abuse of power, overweening, self-indulgence, or the cowardice that turns away from the very trial that would ennoble.
cards.desc: A woman in a flowing gown calmly opens or closes the jaws of a great lion; a lemniscate floats above her head; flowers garland the lion's neck.

cards.name: The Hermit
cards.upright: Counsel, knowledge, solicitude, circumspection, prudence. The sage who withdraws into solitude to kindle the inner light and then carries it as a lantern to guide others.
cards.reversed: Conceit, immaturity, shyness, or the hermit who mistakes isolation for wisdom and so misleads those who seek him.
cards.desc: A hooded old man stands on an icy peak holding aloft a six-pointed lantern, his staff in the other hand, head bowed in contemplation.

cards.name: Wheel of Fortune
cards.upright: Fortune, luck, felicity, the wheel that turns for everyone in time. An unexpected windfall or the recognition that one has entered a new phase of destiny.
cards.reversed: Increase, abundance, but also the downward turn of fortune; some evil, mishap, or the recognition that the wheel cannot be stalled.
cards.desc: An eight-spoked wheel turns within clouds, ringed by an ape, a sphinx, and a jackal; Hebrew and alchemical glyphs mark the rim.

cards.name: Justice
cards.upright: Equity, rightness, probity, executive power, the triumph of the deserving. The balance held by the eternal judge that weighs motive and consequence alike.
cards.reversed: Law in all its phases, legal complications, bias, prejudice, severity in the undeserved, or injustice under the form of legality.
cards.desc: A crowned figure sits on a stone throne between two pillars, holding an upright sword in one hand and balanced scales in the other.

cards.name: The Hanged Man
cards.upright: Self-sacrifice, the reversal of the natural order, a voluntary surrender for the sake of higher understanding. The wisdom that comes from seeing the world upside down.
cards.reversed: Egoism, the body trying to rule the spirit, or the unwilling sacrifice that resists and so loses its redemptive power.
cards.desc: A man hangs by one ankle from a T-shaped gibbet, head haloed, hands bound behind his back forming an inverted triangle; his face is serene.

cards.name: Death
cards.upright: End of an old cycle, transformation, passage from one mode of life to another, the necessary death of that which no longer serves. Rarely a literal death.
cards.reversed: Inertia, sleep, lethargy, petrifaction, somnolence, or the ending that is resisted and so prolonged into stagnation.
cards.desc: A skeleton in black armor rides a pale horse, carrying a banner bearing the white rose; a king lies fallen, a child, bishop, and maiden attend.

cards.name: Temperance
cards.upright: Economy, moderation, frugality, management, accommodation. The angel who mixes fire and water, finding the middle path between all extremes.
cards.reversed: Things connected with religion, the church, discord, bad combination, interests in confused contention, or the virtue turned to sterility.
cards.desc: A winged angel in a white robe with a sun-crown stands with one foot in water and one on land, pouring liquid between two golden cups; irises bloom nearby.

cards.name: The Devil
cards.upright: Violence, vehemence, extraordinary efforts, force, fatality, that which is predestined but not evil in itself. The chains that bind are loosely hung — the captives could slip free.
cards.reversed: Evil fatality, weakness, pettiness, blindness, or the liberating recognition of one's own complicity in bondage.
cards.desc: A horned, bat-winged devil perches on a black cubical altar, an inverted pentagram on his brow; two small horned demons stand chained yet loosely to his pedestal.

cards.name: The Tower
cards.upright: Ruin, disruption, overthrow, the tearing down of false structures built upon sand. The lightning-flash of truth that liberates even as it destroys.
cards.reversed: Negligence, absence, distribution, the same overthrow in a lesser degree, or the catastrophe narrowly averted but the lesson unlearned.
cards.desc: A tall stone tower on a rocky crag is struck by lightning and bursts; flames spout from its windows; two crowned figures tumble headlong into the void.

cards.name: The Star
cards.upright: Hope, faith, unexpected help, clarity of vision, the light that shines after the tower falls. The naked soul pours forth the waters of life under the host of heaven.
cards.reversed: Arrogance, haughtiness, pessimism, or the hope that is ungrounded and so dissolves into mere wishing.
cards.desc: A luminous eight-pointed star shines above a kneeling maiden who pours water from two vessels, one onto the land and one back into the pool; a bird sits in a tree.

cards.name: The Moon
cards.upright: Hidden enemies, danger, error, deception, the twilight path that leads through the land of dreams and ancestral memory. The light that is reflected, not direct.
cards.reversed: Instability, inconstancy, silence, the lesser of two evils, or the deception that dissolves when brought into the open.
cards.desc: A full moon face shedding dew stands between two towers on a winding path; a dog and a wolf howl beneath it while a crayfish rises from a pool.

cards.name: The Sun
cards.upright: Happiness, contentment, joy, felicity, the soul come into its own golden inheritance. The child who rides out under a radiant sun, banner flying, sunflowers on the wall.
cards.reversed: The same in a lesser sense, or the happiness that is fleeting, postponed, or shadowed by minor melancholy.
cards.desc: A great radiant sun fills the sky above a stone wall topped with sunflowers; a joyous naked child rides a white horse, a scarlet banner in hand.

cards.name: Judgement
cards.upright: Change of position, renewal, the outcome of the past, the final reckoning that calls the dead from their graves to rise transformed. A vocational summons.
cards.reversed: Weakness, cowardice, stupidity, the summoning trumpet unheard or refused, or delay in the sentence that would set one free.
cards.desc: An angel with golden wings blows a great trumpet in a radiant sky from which a cross-bearing flag streams; below, three naked figures — man, woman, and child — rise from open coffins.

cards.name: The World
cards.upright: Perfection, consummation, success, triumph, the journey made whole. The dancer within the wreath, the four creatures at the corners, the cosmos realized in a single form.
cards.reversed: Inertia, fixedness, the same perfection in a lesser sense, or the success that closes a door and leaves no road forward.
cards.desc: A naked dancer wreathed in greenery floats within an oval garland; at the four corners the lion, eagle, bull, and angel watch her.

cards.name: Ace of Wands
cards.upright: Creation, invention, the beginning of an enterprise, the source of fiery will. The hand of power reaching from a cloud to offer the living staff, budding with leaves.
cards.reversed: Fall, decadence, ruin, the spark that is smothered, or inspiration that flickers and dies for want of fuel.
cards.desc: A hand emerges from a cloud clasping a living wand sprouting green leaves; a castle and turrets rise on a distant hill.

cards.name: Two of Wands
cards.upright: Between the alternatives, the world in your hand; the lord who surveys his domain and weighs which road to march. Riches, fortune, magnificence.
cards.reversed: Sadness, trouble, the surprise of the unforeseen, or the fear that keeps one from setting sail toward what the globe reveals.
cards.desc: A nobleman stands on a battlement holding a globe of the world and a wand; a second wand is lashed to the wall; the sea and distant shore stretch beyond.

cards.name: Three of Wands
cards.upright: He has sent his ships out and now waits, watching for their return. Established strength, enterprise, effort, trade, the merchant who has launched his venture.
cards.reversed: The end of troubles, cessation of adversity, or the ventures that return empty and must be sent out again with better courage.
cards.desc: A robed merchant stands on a cliff leaning on a wand, watching three merchant ships sail upon a golden sea between two flanking wands.

cards.name: Four of Wands
cards.upright: A garlanded gateway in the country, prosperity, peace, concord. They are the country people dancing beneath the wreath; harvest-home, the fruit of toil made manifest.
cards.reversed: Prosperity in a lesser sense, or the celebration that is short-lived, prosperity and beauty that are nonetheless present.
cards.desc: Four wands topped with garlands form a canopy before a castle; two maidens raise nosegays while others dance in the background.

cards.name: Five of Wands
cards.upright: Imitation, sport, sham fight, a keen contest, the testing of strength that sharpens all who enter. Not deadly war, but the friction of equals contending.
cards.reversed: Litigation, lawsuits, the contest turned bitter, or the reconciliation that ends the quarrel and clears the air.
cards.desc: Five youths wield wands in mock combat, clashing in a tangle of staves under a yellow sky.

cards.name: Six of Wands
cards.upright: A horseman crowned with laurel rides at the head of a procession, a wreath of victory on his wand. Public recognition, success, the laurels fairly won.
cards.reversed: Apprehension, fear, the victor who fears envy, or fame that is undeserved, stolen, or inflated beyond its merit.
cards.desc: A laurel-crowned rider on a white horse carries a wand wreathed in green; foot soldiers march behind him, one bearing a second wreath.

cards.name: Seven of Wands
cards.upright: The holder of the high ground defends his position against many. Valor, discussion, wordy strife, negotiations, trade treaties, the struggle to maintain a hard-won place.
cards.reversed: Perplexity, embarrassments, anxiety, the position that cannot be held, or yielding what was so dearly won.
cards.desc: A youth on a hilltop raises his wand against six attackers below, their wands raised toward him; he holds the advantage of height.

cards.name: Eight of Wands
cards.upright: Activity, swiftness, the arrows of fire flying through the air. News, letters, messengers, the rapid approach of what has long been awaited.
cards.reversed: Thorns of dispute, domestic quarrels, jealousy, the messages that arrive too late or bring discord.
cards.desc: Eight wands streak like arrows through a clear sky above a river, a meadow, and a small hilltop house.

cards.name: Nine of Wands
cards.upright: Order, discipline, strength yet to bear, the wounded soldier who nonetheless keeps watch. Defense against persistent attack, the resolve of one who has endured much.
cards.reversed: Obstacles, adversity, calamity, or the strength that finally fails because the watch was kept too long.
cards.desc: A bandaged soldier leans wearily on a wand beside a palisade of eight other wands; his expression is alert and unyielding.

cards.name: Ten of Wands
cards.upright: A man bears ten heavy staves toward a town. Fortitude, the weight of responsibility, a card of the highest import, the burden carried for the sake of duty.
cards.reversed: Deceit, treachery, perfidy, the burden that crushes, or the relief of setting down what should never have been carried alone.
cards.desc: A bent figure struggles toward a distant town under a heavy load of ten bound wands, almost at the gate.

cards.name: Page of Wands
cards.upright: A young person standing in the open country, holding a wand upright, looking up. Dark young person, faithful, a lover, an envoy, a zealous and ardent messenger.
cards.reversed: Courage in lesser degree, indecision, vacillation, or one who brings news but cannot follow it through.
cards.desc: A gaily-dressed page stands alone in a barren landscape holding a live wand upright, his cap bearing a long fluttering feather.

cards.name: Knight of Wands
cards.upright: Dark young man, active, energetic, ardent, the sudden arrival or departure that changes everything, a journey taken in haste and high spirits.
cards.reversed: Division, interruption, discord, the fire that consumes without illuminating, or the rider who promises much but vanishes.
cards.desc: An armored knight on a rearing horse brandishes a wand as he charges; his tunic is patterned with salamanders.

cards.name: Queen of Wands
cards.upright: Dark woman, friendly, chaste, loving, honorable. She is the ruler of the domestic sphere whose warmth draws all creatures; the cat at her feet betokens her independence.
cards.reversed: Good, economical, obliging, or jealousy and deceit, the warmth that burns, the will that becomes tyranny.
cards.desc: A black-cat and sunflower-bearing queen sits upon a throne carved with lions; she holds a live wand and a sunflower, her eyes forward.

cards.name: King of Wands
cards.upright: Dark man, kind, honest, conscientious, married, the father who rules with fire and warmth. He is the agriculturalist, the artisan, the man who builds and lights at once.
cards.reversed: Good but severe, austere, strict yet tolerant, or the king whose fire has turned to cruelty and tyranny.
cards.desc: A crowned king in flame-patterned robes sits on a throne carved with lions and salamanders; a live wand rests in his hand, a salamander curls at his feet.

cards.name: Ace of Cups
cards.upright: Joy, contentment, abundance, fertility, the overflowing cup. The hand of grace emerging from a cloud offers the chalice from which living water streams; the dove descends bearing a host.
cards.reversed: Mingled joy, alteration, the ebb of the inflowing tide, or love that is withheld, the cup drained before its time.
cards.desc: A hand emerging from a cloud holds a great chalice; a dove bearing a wafer descends into it; five streams of water pour to the lilies below.

cards.name: Two of Cups
cards.upright: Love, friendship, union, the chalice raised between two who pledge each other. The caduceus beside the lion's head betokens the harmony of opposites made one.
cards.reversed: Crossed desires, false love, misunderstanding, the cup raised but not truly shared, or the reconciliation that must be rebuilt.
cards.desc: A youth and maiden each raise a cup toward the other; above them a winged lion's head sheds light and a caduceus twined with serpents rises between.

cards.name: Three of Cups
cards.upright: Maidens in a garden with uplifted cups, success, conquest, the harvest of happy companionship. The arts, music, the sisterhood of shared feeling.
cards.reversed: Excess, feasting without measure, the celebration that turns to dissipation, or the friendship that cools after the cup is drained.
cards.desc: Three maidens in floral garlands dance together, each raising a cup; ripe fruit and vegetables lie on the ground at their feet.

cards.name: Four of Cups
cards.upright: A youth seated beneath a tree, arms folded, considering three cups while a fourth is offered by a hand from a cloud. Blended pleasure, weariness of spirit, the gift unnoticed.
cards.reversed: Novelty, presage, new instruction, the cup finally taken, or the weariness that breaks before a new appetite for life.
cards.desc: A youth sits beneath a tree with three cups before him and arms folded; a cloud-hand offers a fourth cup which he does not notice.

cards.name: Five of Cups
cards.upright: A dark figure in a black cloak looks down at three spilled cups while two upright ones stand ignored behind him. Loss, but loss in part — that which remains is the true lesson.
cards.reversed: Return, news, reunion, the bitter cup that is at last accepted, or the fortune recovered through the kinship of the lost.
cards.desc: A black-cloaked figure stands beside a river under a bridge and castle, mourning three spilled cups; two full cups stand upright behind him unnoticed.

cards.name: Six of Cups
cards.upright: Children in an old garden, their cups filled with flowers, a kind reminiscence, things from the past returned. The card of living memories, of gifts generously given.
cards.reversed: The future, that which is coming, the new generation, or the nostalgia that becomes a refusal to grow into the present.
cards.desc: A boy offers a cup of flowers to a little girl in a walled garden of stylized houses; a sentinel with a lance watches beyond.

cards.name: Seven of Cups
cards.upright: A shadowy figure contemplates seven cups each holding a different vision — a castle, jewels, a wreath, a serpent, a dragon, a head, a shrouded form. Fancy, mirage, the need to choose well.
cards.reversed: Desire, will, determination, resolution, or the dream dispelled by clear sight, the true good distinguished from the glittering false.
cards.desc: A dark figure gazes at seven cups floating in a mist, each containing a different emblem: a castle, jewel, wreath, dragon, serpent, head, shrouded figure.

cards.name: Eight of Cups
cards.upright: A hooded figure turns away from a carefully stacked pile of cups and walks toward distant mountains under an eclipsing moon. The abandoning of a success that has ceased to satisfy, the search for higher meaning.
cards.reversed: Feast, joy, mildness, tepidity, the return to the abandoned cups, or the journey deferred because the leaving seemed too costly.
cards.desc: A cloaked pilgrim with a staff walks away from eight neatly stacked cups toward a pass between two tall cliffs; an eclipsed sun-or-moon hangs low.

cards.name: Nine of Cups
cards.upright: A fat, contented man sits before a curved bench on which nine cups are arrayed as if on display. Concord, happiness, victory, success — the wish card, the cup of fullness.
cards.reversed: Mistakes, material loss, the truth of the situation, or the wish that comes true and is found empty, vanity that is satisfied but not fulfilled.
cards.desc: A prosperous, smiling figure sits cross-legged before an arcade of nine cups arranged in rows, arms folded with satisfaction.

cards.name: Ten of Cups
cards.upright: Children dancing, the rainbow of cups above their home, contentment, repose of the heart. The lasting happiness that comes from right relationship; the home blessed.
cards.reversed: Repose of the heart in lesser degree, or the apparent harmony that hides a private grief, the rainbow that fades.
cards.desc: A man and woman with arms raised stand beneath a great rainbow of ten cups; two children dance joyously between them; a riverside house and garden lie beyond.

cards.name: Page of Cups
cards.upright: A youth stands by the sea, a fish rising from a cup held in his hands. Fair young person, a lover, a faithful servant, an artist touched by the muse, the surprising message.
cards.reversed: Taste, inclinations, the news of the inward life, or one whose gentleness is weakness, who cannot hold what the cup offers.
cards.desc: A gaily-dressed page stands on a shore holding a cup from which a small fish peers out; the sea and a ship are behind him.

cards.name: Knight of Cups
cards.upright: Arrival, approach, an advance — the bringer of news or of love. Graceful, amiable youth, a dreamer who nonetheless rides forward, the poet on horseback.
cards.reversed: Subtlety, artifice, fraud, swindling, or one whose dreams outrun his deeds, the cup of fine words that hide an empty heart.
cards.desc: A graceful knight in fish-and-cup-patterned tunic rides an ambling white horse, holding a cup raised as in offering; a river winds through a barren landscape.

cards.name: Queen of Cups
cards.upright: Fair woman, honest, devoted, loving, intelligent. The queen whose throne is by the sea, the closed cup in her hands betokening the inner life kept whole and sacred.
cards.reversed: Good woman, but not to be trusted, dissolute, a deceitful person of illegitimate birth, or the heart that overflows its bounds and drowns its judgment.
cards.desc: A fair queen sits on a water-carved throne by the sea, holding an ornate covered cup; the waves before her are strangely calm.

cards.name: King of Cups
cards.upright: Fair man, a man of business, law, or divinity, kind, considerate, willing to take responsibility. The king whose ship rides the tossing sea while he remains calm, the cup balanced in his hand.
cards.reversed: Good but avaricious, disposed to drink, or one whose calm is a mask for suppressed emotion, the cup that spills its hidden turbulence.
cards.desc: A bearded king on a throne carved with fish and shells rides upon a great ship on a tossing sea; he holds a cup and sceptre, his bearing serene.

cards.name: Ace of Swords
cards.upright: Triumph, the excessive degree thereof, the great force in all directions. Conquest, the sword that cuts through illusion, the single clear thought that decides all.
cards.reversed: The same in a lesser sense, tyranny of the mind, or the seed of dispute sown by an ill-considered word.
cards.desc: A hand emerging from a cloud holds an upright sword; a crown drops from the point while palm and olive branch float on either side.

cards.name: Two of Swords
cards.upright: Conformity, the balance of two forces, peace restored. A blindfolded woman sits at the water's edge holding two swords crossed at her breast; the moon is behind her, the heart is fenced.
cards.reversed: Falsehood, dishonor, duplicity, the removal of the blindfold, or the truce that was never true and now gives way.
cards.desc: A blindfolded woman in white sits on a stone bench by the sea, two long swords crossed before her heart; the moon hangs low over the water.

cards.name: Three of Swords
cards.upright: Removal, absence, delay, division, rupture, the swords that pierce the heart. A necessary sorrow through which truth is learned; the rain that falls on the broken thing.
cards.reversed: Distraction, confusion, error, loss, or the wound that begins at last to close and the heart to mend.
cards.desc: Three swords pierce a red heart under a stormy, raining sky; nothing else softens the stark design.

cards.name: Four of Swords
cards.upright: Vigilance, retreat, solitude, the hermit's repose, exile, the knight asleep upon his tomb. Healing rest, the mind that lays down its sword and gathers itself.
cards.reversed: Economy, circumspection, management of affairs, the awakening from convalescence, or the rest prolonged into stagnation.
cards.desc: A knight in full armor lies as if entombed upon a sarcophagus, hands in prayer; three swords hang on the wall above and one lies beneath him; a stained-glass window depicts the Virgin.

cards.name: Five of Swords
cards.upright: Infamy, dishonor, degradation, the man who gathers the swords of the vanquished. A victory that is hollow, won at the cost of esteem and fellowship.
cards.reversed: Uncertainty, perplexity, loose dealing, the opponents reconciled, or the hollow victory that returns to humble its taker.
cards.desc: A sneering victor gathers three swords from the ground while two defeated foes walk away; a stormy sea and jagged cliffs stretch behind.

cards.name: Six of Swords
cards.upright: A ferryman poles a boat across calm water, a veiled mother and child huddling within, six swords planted upright in the prow. A passage, a journey away from trouble toward calmer shores.
cards.reversed: Embarrassment, the same passage in lesser sense, or the journey that stalls before reaching the better land.
cards.desc: A boatman poles a flat ferry across still water; a veiled woman and child sit within; six swords stand upright in the vessel's bow.

cards.name: Seven of Swords
cards.upright: Design, attempt, wish, hope, confidence, the man who steals away five of the seven swords from the enemy's camp. Shrewdness, prudence, sometimes a necessary cunning.
cards.reversed: Good advice, counsel, oratory, flattery, or the plot discovered and brought to nothing, the swords recovered.
cards.desc: A figure creeps away from an orange-striped tent carrying five swords; two swords remain planted upright behind him; figures in the distance confer.

cards.name: Eight of Swords
cards.upright: Bad news, conflict, censure, the woman bound and blindfolded amid a cage of swords. The self-imposed prison, the limitations felt as real though the bonds are loose and the castle near.
cards.reversed: Disquiet, difficulty, accident, treachery, or the unexpected freedom, the bands that fall away, the old fetter broken.
cards.desc: A blindfolded woman in a flowing gown stands bound on a muddy shore, surrounded by eight swords planted upright; a distant castle rises on a hill.

cards.name: Nine of Swords
cards.upright: Death, failure, miscarriage, delay, deception, the sorrow that wakes one in the night. Yet the swords hang on the wall and not in the body — the grief is greater in the mind than in the fact.
cards.reversed: Suspicions, mistrust, reasonable fears, shame, or the worst that was feared shown to be less than it seemed.
cards.desc: A figure sits bolt upright in bed, face buried in hands; nine swords hang on the wall behind; a quilt bears the design of roses and the zodiac.

cards.name: Ten of Swords
cards.upright: Affliction, tears, sadness, desolation. Yet the dawn breaks over the sea and the worst is over — the ten swords pin the figure to the ground, but the ending brings renewal.
cards.reversed: Profit, success, advantage, or the same affliction in lesser degree, a fatal blow that nonetheless fails to find its mark.
cards.desc: A figure lies face-down at dawn pierced by ten swords; a black sea and yellow dawn stretch behind; the face is calm rather than anguished.

cards.name: Page of Swords
cards.upright: Authority, watching, spying, examining, the spying youth who holds the sword upright and ready. A diplomatic, watchful, graceful but unpredictable person.
cards.reversed: Unexpected in the管家 manner of a youth, malice, a sickness, or one who speaks and sees more than he ought, the vigilant turned snoop.
cards.desc: A gaily-dressed youth on a windy hill holds a sword upright, hair and cape blown back; birds wheel in a turbulent sky above a distant grove.

cards.name: Knight of Swords
cards.upright: Skill, bravery, capacity, defense, address, enmity, wrath. The dark horse and charging rider who bear down upon their goal, sword raised, haste and daring personified.
cards.reversed: Imprudence, incapacity, puerility, or the rider whose charge carries him past his goal into disaster, folly in action.
cards.desc: An armored knight on a dark rearing horse charges forward at full gallop, sword high; his horse's mane and his cape stream behind in the wind.

cards.name: Queen of Swords
cards.upright: Widowhood, female sadness, sterility, deprivation. A subtle, dark woman, Sorrow has taught her; her judgments are clear, stern, and just — the widow whose sword is her own.
cards.reversed: Malice, error, prejudice, delusion, or the sternness that has soured into bitterness and cold cruelty.
cards.desc: A stern-faced queen on a stone throne between clouds and birds holds an upright sword before her; her hand is raised as if to command silence.

cards.name: King of Swords
cards.upright: Whatsoever arises out of the idea of judgment and all its connections — power, command, authority, militant intelligence, the law-giver and the judge.
cards.reversed: Cruelty, perversity, barbarity, perfidy, or the judge corrupted, the authority wielded to wound rather than to order.
cards.desc: A stern bearded king on a throne carved with butterflies and the winged figure of victory holds an upright sword; his cape bears clouds and birds; the wind blows his hair.

cards.name: Ace of Pentacles
cards.upright: Perfect contentment, felicity, ecstasy, speedy intelligence, gold. The hand of providence offers the coin of the earth; the garden gate stands open behind the lilies.
cards.reversed: The evil side of wealth, misery, purse-proud arrogance, or the gift that is misvalued, the seed not planted.
cards.desc: A hand emerging from a cloud holds a great pentacle; below, a garden path leads through an archway of roses and lilies to a mountain landscape.

cards.name: Two of Pentacles
cards.upright: Embarrassment, difficulty, the need to keep two things going at once. The figure of the dancing youth who tosses two coins in their endless lemniscate while ships ride the tossing sea.
cards.reversed: Enforced gaiety, simulated enjoyment, letters, messages, or the juggling act failing, the balls that fall.
cards.desc: A capering youth juggles two pentacles linked by an infinity sign; behind him two small ships ride the swell of a turbulent sea.

cards.name: Three of Pentacles
cards.upright: Trade, skilled labor, renown, glory, the master craftsman who consults with monk and patron. Work that builds toward a recognized and worthy structure.
cards.reversed: Mediocrity, puerility, pettiness, weakness, or the work that falls short of the plan, the cathedral unfinished.
cards.desc: A sculptor on a bench converses with a robed monk and a hooded patron within an archway; three pentacles are carved on the wall above; a great cathedral rises.

cards.name: Four of Pentacles
cards.upright: The surety of possessions, cleverness in investment, clever holding. A figure clutches a coin to his chest, another crowns his head, two more are planted beneath his feet — the lover of security.
cards.reversed: Disquietude, apprehension, jealousy, suspicion, or the miser's grip that loosens into loss, the security that was illusory.
cards.desc: A crowned figure crouches on a stone seat clutching a pentacle to his chest; one balances on his head, two are planted beneath his feet; a city rises behind.

cards.name: Five of Pentacles
cards.upright: Material trouble, destitution, the two beggars who pass a warm, lit window on which five pentacles are set as ornaments. Yet the door of the sanctuary is open — the help exists.
cards.reversed: Disorder, confusion, ruin, discord, profligacy, or the relief of the trouble at last, the door opened to those who were outside.
cards.desc: Two ragged cripples stumble through falling snow past a stained-glass window glowing with warmth, on which five pentacles are mounted; the church door is nearby.

cards.name: Six of Pentacles
cards.upright: Plenty, gain, presents, gifts, the merchant who weighs his coins and gives to those who kneel. The scale held in equal balance — giving and receiving in right proportion.
cards.reversed: Covetousness, jealousy, illusion, debts paid, gifts refused, or the gift that binds the receiver, the charity that humiliates.
cards.desc: A wealthy merchant in red robes holds a balance in one hand and dispenses coins to two kneeling beggars with the other; six pentacles are arranged along his robe.

cards.name: Seven of Pentacles
cards.upright: Money, finance, much money, the gardener who leans on his hoe and surveys the pentacles growing on the vine. The pause in which the harvest is weighed before the next planting.
cards.reversed: Cause for anxiety, impatience, uneasiness, the harvest that disappoints, or the realization that what was sown must be sown anew.
cards.desc: A young farmer leaning on a hoe gazes thoughtfully at six pentacles growing on a leafy bush, a seventh pentacle on the ground at his feet; a cottage stands behind.

cards.name: Eight of Pentacles
cards.upright: Work, employment, commission, dexterity, the apprentice at the bench engraving his pentacles one by one. Patient skill, work well done, the building of mastery.
cards.reversed: Voided ambition, avarice, jealousy, intrigue, card-playing, or the work hastily done, the craft that is not honored.
cards.desc: A young engraver sits on a bench before a workshop, hammering a pentacle; six finished pentacles are mounted on the wall, another hangs from his bench; a town lies behind.

cards.name: Nine of Pentacles
cards.upright: Prudence, safety, success, accomplishment, the solitary woman in her beautiful garden, the falcon on her wrist, the grapes heavy on the vine. Well-being earned by one's own hand.
cards.reversed: Roguery, safeguard, loss of money, or the abundance that hides an emptiness, the garden beautiful but solitary at its core.
cards.desc: An elegant woman in a floral gown stands in a vineyard with a hooded falcon on her wrist; nine pentacles are scattered on her gown; a snail crawls at her feet, a grand house beyond.

cards.name: Ten of Pentacles
cards.upright: Gain, riches, family, house, dwelling, antiquity, the multi-generational wealth. The old man wrapped in his robe, his dogs at his feet, his children's children playing beneath the arches.
cards.reversed: Loss, robbery, family dispersion, or the inheritance that becomes a burden, the house divided against itself.
cards.desc: An old patriarch in a long robe sits before an archway decorated with ten pentacles; a young couple, child, and two white dogs play within a courtyard; banners hang on the castle wall.

cards.name: Page of Pentacles
cards.upright: Dark youth, scholar, reflective. Application, learning, understanding, news, messages, the student who contemplates the coin he holds with reverence.
cards.reversed: Prodigality, dissipation, liberality, luxury, unfavorable news, or the student who loses interest, the seed of ambition unwatered.
cards.desc: A young man in earth-toned tunic stands alone in a flowery meadow gazing reverently at a pentacle he holds up before him.

cards.name: Knight of Pentacles
cards.upright: Dark young man, active, lithe, full of self-importance, the plodding but reliable knight. He rides his heavy horse slowly through the worked field, his pentacle held firm.
cards.reversed: Stagnation, stay-at-home tendencies, idleness, the cautious turned coward, the diligent turned dull, or the trustworthiness that fails at the critical moment.
cards.desc: An armored knight on a heavy draft horse stands still amid a ploughed field, holding a pentacle level before him; his horse wears trappings of green.

cards.name: Queen of Pentacles
cards.upright: Dark woman, grave, intelligent, chaste, virtuous. She sits in a beautiful garden, her face turned to the bird on her hand, the rabbit at her feet — prosperity with warmth.
cards.reversed: Interdiction, depreciation, unworthy woman of equivocal speech, or the prosperity that becomes possessiveness, the nurturing turned smothering.
cards.desc: A crowned queen on an ornate throne set in a lush garden holds a pentacle on her lap; a rabbit crouches nearby; roses, trees, and a river fill the background.

cards.name: King of Pentacles
cards.upright: Dark man, strong, successful, practical, gifted in business. He sits in his robe of vine-leaves amid the harvest, the bull at his feet, the castle of his estate behind him.
cards.reversed: Avarice, prejudices, plans endangered by their author, or the success that turns to complacency, the king grown fat upon his throne.
cards.desc: A bearded king in vine-patterned robes sits on a throne carved with bulls; he holds a sceptre and a pentacle; a bull and asses are at his feet, a castle and vineyard beyond.
